name: Fight Colorectal Cancer
mission: Learn what colorectal cancer is, how it develops, and the key facts, symptoms, and screening guidelines to know.
theoryOfChange: Fight Colorectal Cancer has a published strategic framework called "Path to a Cure." This document is described as their "visionary roadmap for the future of colorectal cancer research" and the "engine of Fight CRC's research advocacy and strategic planning." It is structured around four pillars, each detailing "progress indicators, key messages, opportunities and challenges, and the voice of survivors." The organization publishes an annual "Progress Report" to score these indicators and demonstrate accountability, aiming to "shorten the timeline between discovery and impact" by aligning priorities and rallying partners.
scoreSummary: Fight Colorectal Cancer shows strong impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 100% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

dimension_explanations.alignment: While the cause of global health and cancer advocacy is urgent, the organization has a low donor fit for those seeking specific faith-based alignment [8]. As a sadaqah-eligible entity, it provides a transparent pathway for general charitable giving but lacks specialized religious giving policies [1][8].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high effectiveness in its operations, allocating an 85.2% program expense ratio toward patient education and research [1][2]. Its financial health is robust, maintaining 12.0 months of working capital to ensure long-term stability in its advocacy efforts [5].

baseline_narrative.headline: Fight Colorectal Cancer empowers a global community of survivors and advocates to advance medical research and public policy.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 85.2% ensures the majority of funds support core missions
Excellent fundraising efficiency with only $0.07 spent per $1 raised
Strong liquidity with 12.0 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: Fight Colorectal Cancer is a national advocacy organization dedicated to supporting patients through education, awareness, and legislative action [1][8]. The organization reported a total revenue of $3,060,301 and maintains a strong focus on capacity building within the colorectal cancer community [2][5].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize systemic change through legislative advocacy and want to support the colorectal cancer community through a highly transparent, sadaqah-eligible organization.
donor_motivations: Value high financial efficiency and low fundraising overhead
Want to support patient empowerment and policy reform
Prefer organizations with the highest transparency ratings (Candid Platinum)
Are comfortable with capacity-building as a primary impact model
giving_considerations: Impact is measured through policy wins and community reach rather than direct service delivery
Strong financial reserves suggest long-term organizational stability
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for international direct-aid humanitarian projects.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization has a <cite id="19">verified theory of change</cite> and tracks outcome metrics, though it lacks <cite id="13">specific beneficiary counts</cite>.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Fight CRC uses a <cite id="19">documented theory of change</cite> focused on empowering survivors to influence policy and research, leading to better patient outcomes.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that advocacy efforts translate into tangible policy changes and improved patient support systems.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="3">$3.1M revenue</cite> and a <cite id="4">10.9% growth rate</cite>, the organization has significant capacity to expand its advocacy reach.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 85.2%</cite> and spends only <cite id="7">$0.07 per $1 raised</cite>, outperforming many advocacy peers.
Fight CRC holds <cite id="9">12.0 months of working capital</cite>, providing a resilient financial cushion for long-term advocacy campaigns.
The charity holds a <cite id="10">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and <cite id="11">meets all 20 BBB Wise Giving Alliance Standards</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: Fight CRC demonstrates a robust commitment to organizational health and transparency. By maintaining <cite id="11">20 out of 20 BBB standards</cite> and a <cite id="10">Platinum Candid Seal</cite>, they provide donors with high-quality data regarding their goals and results. Their financial model is particularly lean; with <cite id="3">revenue exceeding $3 million</cite>, they have managed to keep <cite id="12">independent board oversight at 100%</cite> while scaling their <cite id="2">Research Advocacy Training & Support (RATS) program</cite>. This efficiency is further evidenced by their <cite id="9">12-month reserve of operating expenses</cite>, which is significantly higher than the industry average, ensuring stability in their mission to <cite id="1">push for better medical policies</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Fight Colorectal Cancer (Fight CRC) is a leading national advocacy organization that <cite id="1">empowers a global community of survivors and advocates</cite> to advance medical research and public policy. Operating primarily within the United States, the organization focuses on <cite id="2">patient and caregiver support, education, and research advocacy training</cite>. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $3,060,301</cite>, demonstrating a steady growth trajectory with a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 10.9%</cite>. Fight CRC distinguishes itself through a high <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 85.2%</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of donor contributions directly fund its advocacy and support initiatives. The organization maintains <cite id="6">excellent fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending only <cite id="7">$0.07 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions. Beyond direct support, Fight CRC acts as a catalyst for broader change by <cite id="8">awarding $186,573 in grants</cite> to partners like the American Cancer Society to further colorectal cancer research and policy goals.
